description: Fix SVG gradient ID collisions when multiple plugin icons render in the same DOM. Plugin icons use generic IDs like 'a' and 'b' for linearGradient definitions. When multiple icons coexist in the side panel, url(#a) references resolve to the wrong gradient (e.g., Jira's inactive gray gradient picks up Confluence's active blue gradient because both use id="a"). The fix namespaces all SVG IDs with the plugin name during the build pipeline, and adds extensive unit tests.

userStories.title: Add namespaceSvgIds function to validate-icon.ts
userStories.description: Add a function that rewrites all id attributes and their url(#...), href="#...", and xlink:href="#..." references in an SVG string, prefixing each ID with a given namespace. Export it for use by the build pipeline and tests.
acceptanceCriteria: namespaceSvgIds is exported from validate-icon.ts
id="a" becomes id="<prefix>-a" for all id attributes in the SVG
url(#a) becomes url(#<prefix>-a) for all matching references
href="#a" becomes href="#<prefix>-a" for all matching references
xlink:href="#a" becomes xlink:href="#<prefix>-a" for all matching references
SVGs with no id attributes pass through unchanged
Passthrough values like fill="none" and fill="currentColor" are not affected
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.ts (add namespaceSvgIds function and export it)

Context:
Multiple plugin icons render simultaneously in the Chrome extension side panel. SVG id attributes are document-global â€” when two SVGs both define id="a", url(#a) resolves to the first definition found in DOM order, not the SVG-local one. This causes Jira's inactive icon (which should be all #999999 gray) to display Confluence's active blue gradient because both icons use id="a" and id="b" for their linearGradient definitions.

Current plugins with ID collisions (from scanning plugins/*/icon.svg):
- confluence: id="a", id="b" â€” uses url(#a), url(#b), AND xlink:href="#a"
- jira: id="a", id="b" â€” uses url(#a), url(#b)
- discord: id="clip0" â€” uses url(#clip0)
- supabase: id="paint0_linear", id="paint1_linear" â€” uses url(#paint0_linear), url(#paint1_linear)

Changes:
Add a new function after the generateDarkIcon function (before the export block at line 728):

```typescript
const namespaceSvgIds = (svgContent: string, prefix: string): string => {
```

The function should:
1. Collect all id values from id="..." attributes using a regex
2. If no IDs found, return the SVG unchanged
3. For each collected ID, replace:
   - `id="<id>"` â†’ `id="<prefix>-<id>"`
   - `url(#<id>)` â†’ `url(#<prefix>-<id>)`
   - `href="#<id>"` â†’ `href="#<prefix>-<id>"`
   - `xlink:href="#<id>"` â†’ `xlink:href="#<prefix>-<id>)"`
4. Escape regex special characters in the ID before building replacement patterns

Add `namespaceSvgIds` to the export block at the bottom of the file (currently line 728-737):
```typescript
export {
  DARK_BG_LUMINANCE,
  generateDarkIcon,
  generateInactiveIcon,
  MAX_ICON_SIZE,
  MIN_ICON_CONTRAST,
  MIN_INACTIVE_GRAY,
  namespaceSvgIds,
  validateIconSvg,
  validateInactiveIconColors,
};
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify any existing functions â€” only add the new function and its export
- The function must handle all reference types found in the real plugin icons: url(#id), href="#id", xlink:href="#id"
- The function must use word boundary (\b) on the id attribute match to avoid matching partial attributes like `grid="..."` â€” use `\bid\s*=\s*"([^"]+)"`
- Regex special chars in IDs must be escaped (IDs like paint0_linear are fine, but the function should be robust)

userStories.title: Add extensive unit tests for namespaceSvgIds
userStories.description: Add comprehensive tests for the namespaceSvgIds function covering all SVG reference types, edge cases, multiple IDs, no IDs, regex-special characters in IDs, and real-world plugin icon scenarios.
acceptanceCriteria: Tests cover: simple id + url(#id) rewriting
Tests cover: xlink:href="#id" rewriting
Tests cover: href="#id" rewriting
Tests cover: multiple IDs in a single SVG
Tests cover: SVG with no IDs returns unchanged
Tests cover: IDs with regex special chars (e.g., containing dots or plus signs)
Tests cover: fill="none", fill="currentColor", and other passthrough values are not affected
Tests cover: real-world Jira icon scenario (linearGradient with id="a" and id="b", url(#a), url(#b))
Tests cover: real-world Confluence icon scenario (linearGradient with xlink:href="#a")
Tests cover: clipPath with id (e.g., id="clip0", url(#clip0))
Tests cover: two different SVGs namespaced with different prefixes produce non-colliding IDs
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/validate-icon.test.ts (add new describe block for namespaceSvgIds)

Context:
The test file already imports from ./validate-icon.js and has a helper `svgWrap` that wraps inner SVG content in a valid SVG tag. Add `namespaceSvgIds` to the import.

Current import block (line 1-11):
```typescript
import { describe, expect, test } from 'vitest';
import {
  DARK_BG_LUMINANCE,
  generateDarkIcon,
  generateInactiveIcon,
  MAX_ICON_SIZE,
  MIN_ICON_CONTRAST,
  MIN_INACTIVE_GRAY,
  validateIconSvg,
  validateInactiveIconColors,
} from './validate-icon.js';
```

Add `namespaceSvgIds` to this import.

The existing svgWrap helper (line 14-15):
```typescript
const svgWrap = (inner: string, viewBox = '0 0 32 32'): string =>
  `<svg xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" viewBox="${viewBox}">${inner}</svg>`;
```

Changes:
Add a new `describe('namespaceSvgIds', ...)` block at the end of the file (after the generateDarkIcon tests that end at line 1348). Include tests for:

1. Basic id + url(#id): `<defs><linearGradient id="grad1"><stop/></linearGradient></defs><rect fill="url(#grad1)"/>` with prefix "myplugin" â†’ id="myplugin-grad1" and url(#myplugin-grad1)

2. xlink:href: `<linearGradient id="a"/><linearGradient xlink:href="#a"/>` â†’ both namespaced

3. href: `<linearGradient id="a"/><use href="#a"/>` â†’ both namespaced

4. Multiple IDs: SVG with id="a" and id="b" both used via url(#a) and url(#b) â†’ both namespaced independently

5. No IDs: `<rect fill="#ff0000"/>` â†’ returned unchanged

6. IDs with regex special chars: e.g., id="a.b+c" â†’ properly escaped and namespaced

7. Passthrough values preserved: `<rect fill="none" stroke="currentColor"/>` with some id present â†’ fill and stroke values unaffected, id still namespaced

8. Real Jira scenario: the actual Jira icon structure with `<defs><linearGradient id="a">...</linearGradient><linearGradient id="b">...</linearGradient></defs><path fill="#2684FF"/><path fill="url(#a)"/><path fill="url(#b)"/>` â†’ after namespacing with "jira", all id="jira-a", url(#jira-a), etc.

9. Real Confluence scenario: include xlink:href="#a" in the gradient definition â†’ after namespacing with "confluence", becomes xlink:href="#confluence-a"

10. clipPath scenario: `<defs><clipPath id="clip0"><rect/></clipPath></defs><g clip-path="url(#clip0)"/>` â†’ namespaced

11. Two SVGs with same IDs namespaced differently: svg1 with id="a" prefix="jira" and svg2 with id="a" prefix="confluence" â†’ jira-a vs confluence-a, no collision

12. Already-namespaced SVG is idempotent-safe: namespacing with prefix "p" an SVG that has id="p-a" â†’ becomes id="p-p-a" (correct behavior â€” the function doesn't try to detect existing prefixes, it always prepends)

Constraints:
- Do NOT modify existing tests â€” only add new ones
- Use the existing svgWrap helper where appropriate
- Use the same test patterns as the rest of the file (describe/test/expect)

userStories.title: Integrate namespaceSvgIds into the build pipeline
userStories.description: Call namespaceSvgIds on all four SVG variants in readAndValidateIcons before returning, using the plugin name as the prefix. Update the function signature to accept the plugin name, and update the call site to pass it.
acceptanceCriteria: readAndValidateIcons accepts a pluginName parameter
All four returned SVG variants (iconSvg, iconInactiveSvg, iconDarkSvg, iconDarkInactiveSvg) have namespaced IDs
The call site in the build command passes the plugin name from pkgJson.name
namespaceSvgIds is imported from ../validate-icon.js in build.ts
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.ts (import namespaceSvgIds, update readAndValidateIcons, update call site)

Context:
The readAndValidateIcons function reads icon SVG files, validates them, auto-generates missing variants, minifies them, and returns an IconResult with four optional SVG strings. The namespacing must happen after all processing (validation, generation, minification) so IDs are namespaced in the final output.

Current import from validate-icon.js (line 35-40):
```typescript
import {
  generateDarkIcon,
  generateInactiveIcon,
  validateIconSvg,
  validateInactiveIconColors,
} from '../validate-icon.js';
```
Add `namespaceSvgIds` to this import.

Current function signature (line 451):
```typescript
const readAndValidateIcons = async (projectDir: string): Promise<IconResult> => {
```
Change to:
```typescript
const readAndValidateIcons = async (projectDir: string, pluginName: string): Promise<IconResult> => {
```

Current return statement (line 576-581):
```typescript
  return {
    iconSvg: minifiedIcon,
    iconInactiveSvg: minifiedInactive,
    iconDarkSvg: minifiedDark,
    iconDarkInactiveSvg: minifiedDarkInactive,
  };
```
Change to:
```typescript
  return {
    iconSvg: namespaceSvgIds(minifiedIcon, pluginName),
    iconInactiveSvg: namespaceSvgIds(minifiedInactive, pluginName),
    iconDarkSvg: namespaceSvgIds(minifiedDark, pluginName),
    iconDarkInactiveSvg: namespaceSvgIds(minifiedDarkInactive, pluginName),
  };
```

Current call site (line 978):
```typescript
  const icons = await readAndValidateIcons(projectDir);
```
The plugin name is available as `pkgJson.name` (line 975 creates `pkgJson` via `validatePackageJson`). Change to:
```typescript
  const icons = await readAndValidateIcons(projectDir, pkgJson.name);
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change any other logic in readAndValidateIcons â€” only the signature, the return statement, and the import
- Do NOT change the IconResult interface
- The namespacing must happen AFTER minification, not before (so IDs are namespaced in the final minified output)

userStories.title: Update readAndValidateIcons tests for the new pluginName parameter
userStories.description: Update all existing readAndValidateIcons test call sites to pass a pluginName argument, and add new tests verifying that returned SVGs contain namespaced IDs.
acceptanceCriteria: All existing readAndValidateIcons calls in tests pass a pluginName argument (e.g., 'test-plugin')
Existing tests still pass with the new parameter
New test verifies auto-generated inactive icon contains namespaced IDs (not the original generic IDs)
New test verifies all four icon variants contain namespaced IDs when icon has linearGradient definitions
New test verifies icons without IDs (e.g., simple fill="#ff0000") are unaffected by namespacing
userStories.notes:
Files:
- platform/plugin-tools/src/commands/build.test.ts (update existing calls, add new tests)

Context:
The readAndValidateIcons tests are in a describe block starting at line 590. All current call sites pass only `tmpDir`:

```typescript
const result = await readAndValidateIcons(tmpDir);
```
and:
```typescript
await expect(readAndValidateIcons(tmpDir)).rejects.toThrow(...);
```

These must all gain a second argument. Use 'test-plugin' as the plugin name for existing tests.

Existing test constants (line 585-588):
```typescript
const VALID_ICON_SVG =
  '<svg viewBox="0 0 32 32" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="16" cy="16" r="12" fill="#ff0000"/></svg>';
const VALID_INACTIVE_SVG =
  '<svg viewBox="0 0 32 32" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><circle cx="16" cy="16" r="12" fill="#808080"/></svg>';
```

Note: VALID_ICON_SVG has no id attributes, so namespacing has no effect â€” existing tests that check for #ff0000 or #808080 will continue to pass.

Changes:
1. Update every `readAndValidateIcons(tmpDir)` call to `readAndValidateIcons(tmpDir, 'test-plugin')` â€” there are 11 call sites in the describe block (lines 602, 608, 621, 630, 635, 641, 650, 658, 669, 678, plus any error-throwing calls)

2. Add a new test: 'namespaces gradient IDs in auto-generated variants'. Create an SVG with a linearGradient:
```typescript
const SVG_WITH_GRADIENT = '<svg viewBox="0 0 32 32" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg"><defs><linearGradient id="g1"><stop stop-color="#ff0000"/></linearGradient></defs><rect fill="url(#g1)"/></svg>';
```
Write it as icon.svg, call readAndValidateIcons with pluginName 'myplugin'. Verify:
- result.iconSvg contains 'id="myplugin-g1"' and 'url(#myplugin-g1)'
- result.iconSvg does NOT contain 'id="g1"'
- result.iconInactiveSvg contains 'id="myplugin-g1"'
- result.iconDarkSvg contains 'id="myplugin-g1"'
- result.iconDarkInactiveSvg contains 'id="myplugin-g1"'

3. Add a test: 'icons without IDs are unaffected by namespacing' â€” use the existing VALID_ICON_SVG (no IDs), verify result.iconSvg still contains '#ff0000' and does not contain 'test-plugin-' anywhere.

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the test logic of existing tests â€” only add the pluginName argument
- Do NOT remove or rewrite existing tests
- The existing VALID_ICON_SVG and VALID_INACTIVE_SVG have no id attributes, so adding the pluginName arg should not change any existing assertions
